subject: Geography
title: Emperor Penguins
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Emperor Penguins.

hook.text: Emperor penguins breed in Antarctica's winter—when temperatures drop to minus 60°C.

buildup.text: Males balance a single egg on their feet for 65 days while fasting in total darkness.

discovery.text: They huddle in groups of thousands, rotating positions so each takes a turn on the cold outer edge.

twist.text: In 2022, satellite images revealed colonies failing as sea ice broke apart before chicks were ready.

climax.text: The most devoted parents in nature face extinction if their icy nursery disappears.

punchline.text: They survive the coldest winter on Earth—but not a warming one.

quiz.question: How long do male emperor penguins incubate their egg?
